item.title: What war on Iran means for Palestine
item.description: SOME are expressing frustration that Iran’s conditions to end the war have not explicitly and unequivocally included a demand to end the Israeli occupation of Palestine and dismantle the apartheid regime....

item.title: Warming ocean and rising cyclone risk
item.description: AS THE planet warms, a quiet but consequential transformation is taking place beneath the surface of the world’s oceans. More than 90 per cent of the excess heat trapped by greenhouse gases has been absorbed by the seas, steadily raising ocean temperatures......

item.title: High growth, high risk
item.description: BANGLADESH’S economic story has long been celebrated as one of resilience and steady progress. Over the past decade, the country has sustained robust GDP growth, expanded its export base and achieved notable gains in poverty reduction. Yet beneath......

item.title: CANAL EXCAVATION: Digging deeper or thinking smarter?
item.description: CANAL excavation or canal restoration is far more than moving earth. It concerns restoring abandoned waterways, so they function again within a dynamic hydrological, geomorphological and ecological system. It means reviving channel form and......

item.title: Wake-up call from measles outbreak
item.description: THE widening measles outbreak, cases being reported from at least 56 of the 64 districts, warrants a genuine review of children’s immunisation state. The outbreak questions the integrity of the national immunisation programme. Within weeks, measles has......

item.title: Parliament must not dilute democratic gains
item.description: THE special parliamentary committee, formed on March 12, when the parliament began its first session, to review the 133 ordinances that the interim administration promulgated, has placed its report, recommending that 98 of the ordinances could......

item.title: MSF condemns ‘appalling’ attack on Sudan hospital
item.description: International doctors’ charity MSF on Friday denounced a drone assault on a Sudanese hospital the day before that killed 10 people, attributing the attack to the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces....

item.title: Pope speaks with Israeli, Ukrainian presidents, urging peace
item.description: Pope Leo XVI spoke on Friday with the presidents of Israel and Ukraine, discussing the on-going wars and the need for peace....

item.title: Easter masses cancelled in Dubai because of war
item.description: All masses in Dubai have been cancelled because of the Middle East war, two Catholic churches in the United Arab Emirates posted on their websites on Friday, just ahead of Easter....

item.title: China urges respect for Palestinian rights after Israel passes death penalty law
item.description: China called on Friday for Palestinians’ rights to be protected after Israel approved a bill to allow the execution of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks....

item.title: Trump gloats on possible war crimes in Iran, but punishment distant
item.description: Threatening to destroy Iran’s electricity grid and to reduce the country of 90 million to destitution, US president Donald Trump is shattering precedent by not just accepting but gloating about acts seen as potential war crimes....

item.title: Paris police ban French Muslim gathering
item.description: The Paris police on Thursday banned an annual gathering of French Muslims north of the capital, citing a ‘major terrorist risk’ that it might be targeted....

item.title: Kim pets puppies, kittens at Pyongyang pet shop
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un petted puppies and kittens with his delighted daughter as they toured a new Pyongyang housing district, state media said Friday....

item.title: WHO warns about ‘multiple attacks on health’ in Iran
item.description: The World Health Organisation warned Friday about ‘multiple attacks on health’ in Iran in recent days, while paying tribute to the Pasteur Institute in the capital which was destroyed in an airstrike....

item.title: Iran’s ex-FM urges deal with US to end war
item.description: Iran should make a deal with the United States to end the war by offering to curb its nuclear programme and reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for sanctions relief, a former Iranian foreign minister said....

item.title: Bangladesh beat India on penalties to regain SAFF U-20 title
item.description: Bangladesh regained the SAFF Under-20 Championship title by beating four-time champions India 4-3 on penalties after the regulation time of the 8th edition final ended goalless at the National Stadium in Male, Maldives, on Friday. ...Human rights reports

item.title: Rashford has ‘big chance’ with Raphinha out, says Flick
item.description: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said winger Marcus Rashford has a ‘big chance’ to impress with Raphinha out injured ahead of Saturday’s visit to face Atletico Madrid in La Liga....

item.title: Culture clash spelt shock end for Japan women’s first foreign coach
item.description: Nils Nielsen’s shock exit as Japan coach after leading them to Women’s Asian Cup glory was partly down to communication issues and cultural differences, officials and experts said....

item.title: PSG return to domestic action
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain are focused on the impending showdown with Liverpool and the defence of their Champions League title as they return to domestic action this weekend against Toulouse....

item.title: Napoli, AC Milan face off as Italy licks WC wounds
item.description: Napoli and AC Milan will face off on Monday with Serie A leaders Inter Milan in their sights, but the return of domestic football over Easter has been completely overshadowed by the fallout from Italy failing to reach yet another World Cup....

item.title: Spain is tolerant, not racist, says Arbeloa
item.description: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa said Friday that Spain was a tolerant country and not racist, despite Islamophobic chanting during a national team match this week....

item.title: Barca need Yamal at best for Atletico ‘trilogy’
item.description: Barcelona winger Raphinha’s untimely injury heaps further pressure on teenage star Lamine Yamal ahead of the club’s triple-header against Atletico Madrid....

item.title: Bangladesh face China today
item.description: Debutant Bangladesh will be looking to secure their first win in the AFC Under-20 Women’s Asian Cup when they take on China in their Group A second match at the Nonthaburi Stadium in Thailand today....

item.title: BCB aims to improve relations with BCCI
item.description: The Bangladesh Cricket Board were in ‘constant communication’ with their Indian counterpart BCCI, in hopes of improving the relations, stated Mokhsedul Kamal, the chairman of the media and communication committee, on Friday. ...

item.title: British Council delegation meets UGC chairman
item.description: A four-member delegation from the British Council, Bangladesh paid a courtesy call on the chairman of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh Professor Mamun Ahmed at the UGC in Dhaka on Thursday....Human rights reports

item.description: The Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology signed a memorandum of understanding with the Pubali Bank PLC at the CUET in Chattogram on Thursday....

item.title: AI can be helpful but never substitute for creativity: Fabio Novembre
item.description: Italian architect and designer Fabio Novembre at a programme in Dhaka has said that artificial intelligence can be helpful, but it is never a substitute for creativity....

item.title: ‘Photoseum: Life of Poetree’ features Shamsur Rahman, Al Mahmud
item.description: The 66th solo exhibition of renowned photographer Nasir Ali Mamun, titled Photoseum: Life of Poetree, featuring photographs of two legendary Bangladeshi poets, Shamsur Rahman and Al Mahmud, inaugurated on Friday at Alliance Française de Dhaka......

item.title: Two-day literary festival begins in Rajshahi
item.description: A two-day literary festival titled ‘Rajshahi Literary Festival 2026’ began in Rajshahi on Friday, bringing together poets, writers, academics and cultural activists in a collective expression against war and aggression....

item.title: Occupied footpaths hinder Rajshahi pedestrians
item.description: Residents of Rajshahi city continue to suffer as most paved footpaths across key urban areas remain illegally occupied, forcing pedestrians onto busy roads and raising serious safety concerns amid weak enforcement and allegations of organised extortion....

item.title: Bangladesh highlights UNGA bid at Toronto reception
item.description: Bangladesh consulate general in Toronto hosted a reception for the consular corps marking the Independence anniversary, highlighting Dhaka’s candidature for the UN General Assembly presidency and strengthening bilateral and multilateral engagement....Human rights reports

item.title: DU Rover Scout celebrates diamond jubilee
item.description: The Dhaka University Rover Scout Group celebrated its diamond jubilee at the Teachers-Students Centre on the university campus on Friday....

item.title: Jamaat-led alliance to hold protest rally in Dhaka today
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance will hold a protest rally in the capital Dhaka today to press for its demand for the implementation of the February 12 referendum verdict by passing the July National Charter in Jatiya Sangsad....

item.title: Markets largely defy directive
item.description: Shopping malls and markets largely defied the government directive to close their outlets by 6:00pm from Friday in a bid to minimise energy consumption amid the ongoing Gulf conflict....

item.title: TIB disappointed over JS body recommendations for repealing ordinances
item.description: Transparency International Bangladesh on Friday expressed deep disappointment over the recommendation of a parliamentary special committee for repealing two ordinances relating to the appointment of Supreme Court judges and......

item.title: Long queues at filling stations persist amid govt denial
item.description: Long queues of motor vehicles at filling stations across major cities persisted on Friday due to supply disruptions, although the government maintained assurance of adequate fuel supply....

item.title: Footpaths, roads reoccupied after eviction
item.description: The footpaths and roads where the Dhaka Metropolitan Police conducted eviction operations to reclaim these to ensure smooth pedestrian and traffic movement are being reoccupied in places amid the drive....Geographic Reference

item.title: BPC in a fix to import refined, crude oils
item.description: State-owned Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation is facing difficulties to maintain imports of crude and refined fuel oils with the rising war in the Persian Gulf, affecting its traditional sources in the Arabian Peninsula, said Energy Division officials....

item.title: 94 measles suspects die in 18 days
item.description: At least three children died and 947 others were hospitalised with measles symptoms across Bangladesh in the 24 hours until 8:00am on Friday, raising the total suspected measles death toll to 94 and hospitalisations to 5,792 since March 15, according to the......

item.title: Coast Guard seizes 37,000 litres of diesel in Ctg
item.description: A joint drive by the Bangladesh Coast Guard and the Bangladesh Navy has seized 37,000 litres of illegally stored diesel from a tanker at Sadarghat in Chattogram on Thursday....
